documentation.readme: Apollo is a Windows agent written in C# using the 4.0 .NET Framework designed to be used in SpecterOps training offerings.
usage_sections: Installation: To install Apollo, you'll need Mythic installed on a remote computer. You can find installation instructions for Mythic at the Mythic project page. From the Mythic install directory, use the following command to install Apollo as the root user: From the Mythic install directory,

arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the c2 category. Fits when the engagement needs: Apollo is a Windows agent written in C# using the 4.0 .NET Framework designed to be used in SpecterOps training offerings.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Apollo is a Windows agent written in C# using the 4.0 .NET Framework designed to be used in SpecterOps training offerings.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `./mythic-cli install github $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL`. Further invocations from upstream: `sudo -E ./mythic-cli install github $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL`. Apollo is a Windows agent written in C# using the 4.0 .NET Framework designed to be used in SpecterOps training offerings.

name: Apollo
summary: Apollo is a Windows agent written in C# using the 4.0 .NET Framework designed to be used in SpecterOps training offerings.

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need c2, use Apollo because Apollo is a Windows agent written in C# using the 4.0 .NET Framework designed to be used in SpecterOps training offerings.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./mythic-cli install github $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL` as documented upstream for Apollo.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sudo -E ./mythic-cli install github $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL` as documented upstream for Apollo.
When chaining c2 tools, pair Apollo with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `Apollo --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
